tasks.title: PRD From Brief
tasks.instruction: Use BRIEF.md as the canonical intake context. Rewrite or enrich PRD.md so it clearly states the problem, the target outcome, the constraints, and the success criteria for this mission. Only modify PRD.md in this task. Do not create or edit SPEC.md, implementation files, verification files, or delivery artifacts. Do not create application code in this task.

causedByRequestId: session.launch:2026-04-21T18:53:48.241Z:{"taskId":"prd/01-prd-from-brief"}
tasks.request-generation:2026-04-21T19:00:04.532Z

tasks.title: Draft Spec
tasks.instruction: Read PRD.md and define the technical target in SPEC.md. The specification must describe the intended design, the key boundaries, and the concrete files that will change. Only modify SPEC.md in this task. Do not create implementation code or verification evidence in this task.

tasks.title: Plan Implementation And Verification
tasks.instruction: Read SPEC.md and turn it into an execution ledger. Create paired markdown task files under 03-IMPLEMENTATION/tasks for each implementation slice: one task for building the slice and a second verification task for testing or validating that same slice. Name the verification task with the "-verify.md" suffix, keep both tasks in 03-IMPLEMENTATION/tasks, and make the verification task depend on its implementation task. The verification task must focus on tests and validation rather than feature creation. Do not create application code in this planning task. Do not modify delivery artifacts.

causedByRequestId: tasks.request-generation:2026-04-21T19:00:04.532Z

tasks.title: Draft Spec
tasks.instruction: Read PRD.md and define the technical target in SPEC.md. The specification must describe the intended design, the key boundaries, and the concrete files that will change. Only modify SPEC.md in this task. Do not create implementation code or verification evidence in this task.

tasks.title: Plan Implementation And Verification
tasks.instruction: Read SPEC.md and turn it into an execution ledger. Create paired markdown task files under 03-IMPLEMENTATION/tasks for each implementation slice: one task for building the slice and a second verification task for testing or validating that same slice. Name the verification task with the "-verify.md" suffix, keep both tasks in 03-IMPLEMENTATION/tasks, and make the verification task depend on its implementation task. The verification task must focus on tests and validation rather than feature creation. Do not create application code in this planning task. Do not modify delivery artifacts.

tasks.request-generation:manual:implementation:2026-04-21T19:26:45.880Z

tasks.title: Build Browser Shell
tasks.instruction: Implement the browser entry shell for the game slice. Create and wire `index.html`, `src/main.js`, and `src/styles/game.css` so the project boots from the browser, mounts a full-viewport canvas, and establishes the base page and HUD layering structure needed by the runtime. Keep this task focused on shell and bootstrap concerns; do not implement renderer internals, gameplay systems, or level content here.

tasks.title: Verify Browser Shell
tasks.instruction: Test and validate the browser shell created in the paired implementation task. Confirm the HTML entrypoint loads the JavaScript bootstrap correctly, the canvas can mount into a full-viewport layout, and the shell supports the intended HUD overlay structure without module-loading or startup errors. Keep this task focused on tests and validation only; do not add new feature work.

tasks.title: Build Engine Systems
tasks.instruction: Implement the platform-facing engine systems in `src/engine/renderer.js`, `src/engine/input.js`, and `src/engine/audio.js`. Establish the WebGL-backed renderer setup and frame lifecycle, normalize keyboard and mouse input into gameplay actions, and expose explicit sound and music playback interfaces that gameplay code can call later. Keep this task centered on engine capabilities and interfaces rather than game-state rules or content authoring.

tasks.title: Verify Engine Systems
tasks.instruction: Test and validate the engine systems from the paired implementation task. Verify WebGL initialization and resize handling, confirm keyboard and mouse events are normalized into the expected action snapshot, and validate that audio interfaces can be invoked through stable, explicit APIs. Keep this task focused on test coverage, smoke checks, and validation evidence instead of adding new engine features.

tasks.title: Build Player State And HUD
tasks.instruction: Implement the core runtime state and player-facing loop in `src/game/state.js`, `src/game/player.js`, and `src/game/hud.js`. Define the shared game-state structures from the spec, add player movement/aim/fire/interact handling against that state, and provide HUD logic for health, armor, ammo, keys, and objective display. Keep this task focused on state ownership and player/HUD behavior, not enemy AI or level progression content.

tasks.title: Verify Player State And HUD
tasks.instruction: Test and validate the state, player, and HUD slice from the paired implementation task. Confirm the implemented state shape matches the spec contracts, validate movement and interaction updates against representative inputs, and verify HUD outputs stay synchronized with health, armor, ammo, keys, and objective state changes. Keep this task strictly on testing and validation rather than feature expansion.

tasks.title: Build Combat And Enemies
tasks.instruction: Implement the combat and enemy simulation systems in `src/game/enemies.js` and `src/game/combat.js`. Add enemy update behavior, pursuit and attack flow, death-state handling, and combat resolution for player fire, damage application, pickups, and other shared encounter effects described by the spec. Keep this task focused on encounter logic and damage resolution, not new shell, renderer, or level-data concerns.

tasks.title: Verify Combat And Enemies
tasks.instruction: Test and validate the combat and enemy systems from the paired implementation task. Verify enemy update transitions, attack timing, and death handling, and validate combat resolution for hits, health changes, pickups, and related encounter outcomes. Keep this task focused on tests, simulation checks, and behavioral validation rather than adding more gameplay functionality.

tasks.title: Build Level Progression And Content
tasks.instruction: Implement the data-driven content and progression layer in `src/game/levels/level-01.js`, `src/game/levels/level-02.js`, `src/game/levels/index.js`, and `src/assets/manifest.js`. Define the first two level data modules, add the level registry and loader path, and prove the structured gameplay loop for spawn, combat pressure, keycard collection, door unlock, exit, and transition into the next level while keeping content definitions separate from engine code. Keep this task focused on reusable level data and progression wiring rather than advanced polish beyond the current spec.

tasks.title: Verify Level Progression And Content
tasks.instruction: Test and validate the multi-level content slice from the paired implementation task. Verify level definitions load through the registry, confirm the keycard, door, exit, and level-transition flow works as specified across the first two levels, and validate that asset manifest references required by the slice are present and internally consistent. Keep this task focused on validation and test evidence only; do not add new content or features here.

Task summary:
Test and validate the combat and enemy systems from the paired implementation task. Keep this task focused on tests, simulation checks, and behavioral validation rather than adding more gameplay functionality.

payload.prompt:
You are working on task '5 Build Level Progression And Content'.
Stay strictly within this mission workspace: /missions/flying-pillow/doom/1-initial-setup
Do not read, modify, or create files outside that folder boundary.
Perform the task exactly as specified in <05-level-progression-and-content.md>.
Here are your instructions: @/missions/flying-pillow/doom/1-initial-setup/.mission/missions/1-initial-setup/03-IMPLEMENTATION/tasks/05-level-progression-and-content.md
That task file is authoritative.

Task summary:
Implement the data-driven content and progression layer in `src/game/levels/level-01.js`, `src/game/levels/level-02.js`, `src/game/levels/index.js`, and `src/assets/manifest.js`. Define the first two level data modules, add the level registry and loader path, and prove the structured gameplay loop for spawn, combat pressure, keycard collection, door unlock, exit, and transition into the next level while keeping content definitions separate from engine code. Keep this task focused on reusable level data and progression wiring rather than advanced polish beyond the current spec.

Task summary:
Test and validate the multi-level content slice from the paired implementation task. Verify level definitions load through the registry, confirm the keycard, door, exit, and level-transition flow works as specified across the first two levels, and validate that asset manifest references required by the slice are present and internally consistent. Keep this task focused on validation and test evidence only; do not add new content or features here.
